summary: Large Kirby copied-neutral-special decomp pass centered on src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNPk.c. It replaced many asm/stub markers with C for Pikachu/Pichu, Koopa/Giga Bowser, Link/Young Link, and Samus-copy neutral-B behavior, plus small header/type fixes. The decomp.dev bot reported +10,360 matched code bytes and 44 new matches; the author explicitly noted one remaining regswap issue in ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim, which the bot showed at 97.36% rather than 100%.
key_files: src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNPk.c - main decomp/matching work; 1019 additions, many formerly stubbed Kirby copied neutral-B functions implemented
src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.h - updated Link-copy helper prototypes from void to bool and exposed ftKb_Init_803CB540
src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/types.h - changed specialn_ss_frames_per_charge_level from u32 to s32
what_changed: Implemented ftKb_PkSpecialAirN_Coll and adjusted Pikachu/Pichu copied neutral-B ground/air collision transitions to preserve animation frame and select ftKb_MS_Pk/Pc motion states.

Implemented Koopa-copy flame spawning and air collision handlers: ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA588, ftKb_KpSpecialAirNStart_Coll, ftKb_KpSpecialAirN_Coll, and ftKb_KpSpecialAirNEnd_Coll, including itKoopaFlame_Spawn, random direction table ftKb_Init_803CB540, charge-size SFX choice, and Koopa/Giga Bowser motion-state switches.

Implemented a large Link/Young Link copied bow/arrow block: bow and arrow spawn helpers ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500 and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB5F4 now return bool, ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB6DC fires/releases the arrow, start helper functions ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB880 and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FBA00 enter ground/air start states, and Link start/loop/end Anim/IASA/Coll functions were filled in.

Implemented Samus-copy charge-shot cleanup and firing helpers, including ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCDE0, ftKb_SsSpecialNStart_Anim, ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim, ftKb_SsSpecialAirNStart_Anim, and ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_IASA; charge-shot item/effect cleanup was factored into small static inline helpers.

Reworked several existing functions for matching: changed local motion-state variable types from FtMotionId to s32 in places, moved declarations/scopes, used explicit hat.kind switch cases instead of default fallthroughs, removed/avoided an inline dat_attrs accessor, and added PAD_STACK/dummy stack locals.

Added includes for it/forward.h and it/items/itkoopaflame.h and added extern references for data/constants such as ftKb_Init_804D9420, ftKb_Init_803B7548, ftKb_Init_803CB6C0, ftKb_Init_804D9448, and ftKb_Init_804D9450/804D9454/804D9458.
Updated ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC00 to use extern float constants for its offset/default vector values instead of hardcoded literals.
decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code at 57.96% (+0.27%, +10360 bytes) and matched data at 37.65% (+24 bytes), with 44 new matches and 19 improvements in unmatched items.
smart_moves: The PR made helper return types match control flow: ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500 and ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB5F4 return bool so callers can cleanly bail out and unset bow/arrow state when item spawning fails.

It reused the same hat.kind-driven pattern across copied-special variants: Link vs CLINK, Koopa vs GKOOPS, and Pikachu vs Pichu all choose motion IDs and dat_attrs through small switches rather than duplicating full functions.
The code preserves item ownership state around item operations, notably saving/restoring fp->item_gobj around arrow release and calling ftpickupitem_80094818 after ftKb_SpecialNLk_UnsetArrow.

The Samus-copy cleanup path was decomposed into static inline helpers for charge-shot item cleanup, effect cleanup, and facing-angle conversion, which makes the decompiled behavior readable while still serving matching constraints.
Using extern data symbols for random direction tables, vec groups, sound/offset constants, and sdata2 floats avoided baking in literals that would hurt data/rodata matching.
Collision functions consistently preserve cur_anim_frame and call ftKirbyDmgInline/ftAnim_8006EBA4 where needed after motion changes, keeping Kirby-copy callbacks and animation state aligned.
decomp_lessons: For Kirby copied-special code, expect one source file to contain many character-specific neutral-B variants; matching often requires understanding both Kirby state and the original character's item/state helpers.

Header prototypes should be corrected as soon as decompiled control flow reveals success/failure semantics; the void-to-bool change for Link-copy bow/arrow spawn helpers was necessary for callers and matching.
Use dat_attrs fields rather than hardcoded constants when copied specials differ by source character or clone, such as Link vs Young Link charge speed/max charge/freefall and Samus charge timing.

When collision functions are near-identical ground/air mirrors, preserve the exact ground/air helper pairing: ft_80082708 with ftCommon_8007D5D4 for leaving ground, and ft_80081D0C with ftCommon_8007D7FC for landing/air transitions.
Small signedness fixes in structs can matter: specialn_ss_frames_per_charge_level was changed from u32 to s32, matching the arithmetic/comparisons in Samus charge code.
Be explicit about remaining nonmatches. The PR title says almost 100%, and the body names ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim as the unresolved regswap; future work should not assume it was fully solved.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied-special functions retain the ftKb_ prefix and append abbreviated source-character tags such as Pk, Pc, Kp, Gk, Lk, Cl, and Ss in motion-state/function names.
Address-suffixed helper names such as ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA588, ftKb_SpecialNLk800FB500, and ftKb_SpecialNSs_800FCDE0 were preserved where semantic names were not yet known.
Motion states follow ftKb_MS_<source-copy><ground-or-air><phase>, for example ftKb_MS_LkSpecialNStart, ftKb_MS_ClSpecialAirNLoop, ftKb_MS_KpSpecialAirNEnd, and ftKb_MS_SsSpecialNHold.

DatAttrs fields encode the copied move and source character, such as specialn_lk_arrow_charge_speed, specialn_cl_max_charge, specialn_kp_breath_x_offset, specialn_ss_charge_time, and specialn_ss_frames_per_charge_level.
Small static inline helpers were named semantically where possible, e.g. ftKb_SpecialNSs_DestroyChargeShot, ftKb_ChargeShot_inline, facing_to_angle, and ftKb_SpecialNSs_UnsetChargeShot.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK and dummy u8 stack arrays to reproduce stack frame layout in newly matched functions.
Changed local motion-state variables from enum-like FtMotionId to s32 in several collision paths, likely to influence register allocation/codegen.
Preserved compiler-sensitive declaration scopes and repeated GET_FIGHTER(gobj) calls inside switch blocks instead of always reusing an existing Fighter*.
Used explicit switch cases with empty break bodies for the default source character, rather than relying on default labels, to shape generated code.
Introduced small static inline wrappers for Samus charge-shot cleanup and recoil logic; these inline boundaries can help reproduce original call/branch/register behavior while keeping readable C.
Moved constants into extern sdata2/data symbols such as ftKb_Init_804D9420 and ftKb_Init_804D9450/54/58 to improve data/float matching.
For Link bow animation, copied ftKb_Init_803B7548 Vec3 values into local Vec3 variables and computed atan2f from the local copies, matching the expected access/order pattern.
Author-reported unresolved tactic: ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim still had a register-swap mismatch despite reaching 97.36% match in the bot report.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews were present in the slice.
The author's PR body explicitly called out the remaining issue: "Couldn't figure out the regswap on ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim".
decomp-dev bot feedback confirmed the impact: 44 new matches, 19 unmatched-item improvements, and ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim improved to 97.36% rather than full match.
follow_up_queries: Kirby Samus charge shot ftKb_SpecialNSs cleanup inline matching
